verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem post hæc ut Absalom filii David sororem speciosissimam, vocabulo Thamar, adamaret Amnon filius David,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י אַחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן וּלְאַבְשָׁל֧וֹם בֶּן־דָּוִ֛ד אָח֥וֹת יָפָ֖ה וּשְׁמָ֣הּ תָּמָ֑ר וַיֶּאֱהָבֶ֖הָ אַמְנ֥וֹן בֶּן־דָּוִֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass after this that Ammon the son of David loved the sister of Absalom the son of David, who was very beautiful, and her name was Thamar.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui sitte, että Absalomilla Davidin pojalla oli kaunis sisar, jonka nimi oli Tamar; ja Amnon Davidin poika rakasti häntä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after this, that Absalom the son of David had a fair sister, whose name was Tamar; and Amnon the son of David loved her.

verses.latin_vulgate: et deperiret eam valde, ita ut propter amorem ejus ægrotaret : quia cum esset virgo, difficile ei videbatur ut quippiam inhoneste ageret cum ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֨צֶר לְאַמְנ֜וֹן לְהִתְחַלּ֗וֹת בַּֽעֲבוּר֙ תָּמָ֣ר אֲחֹת֔וֹ כִּ֥י בְתוּלָ֖ה הִ֑יא וַיִּפָּלֵא֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י אַמְנ֔וֹן לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת לָ֖הּ מְאֽוּמָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he was exceedingly fond of her, so that he fell sick for the love of her: for as she was a virgin, he thought it hard to do any thing dishonestly with her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Amnon saatti itsensä tuskissansa sairaaksi sisarensa Tamarin tähden; sillä hän oli neitsy. Ja näkyi työlääksi Amnonin silmäin edessä hänelle jotakin tehdä.
verses.king_james: And Amnon was so vexed, that he fell sick for his sister Tamar; for she was a virgin; and Amnon thought it hard for him to do any thing to her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Amnon amicus nomine Jonadab, filius Semmaa fratris David, vir prudens valde.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְאַמְנ֣וֹן רֵ֗עַ וּשְׁמוֹ֙ יֽוֹנָדָ֔ב בֶּן־שִׁמְעָ֖ה אֲחִ֣י דָוִ֑ד וְי֣וֹנָדָ֔ב אִ֥ישׁ חָכָ֖ם מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now Ammon had a friend, named Jonadab the son of Semmaa the brother of David, a very wise man:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Amnonilla oli ystävä,joka kutsuttiin Jonadab, Simean Davidin veljen poika; ja Jonadab oli sangen kavala mies.
verses.king_james: But Amnon had a friend, whose name was Jonadab, the son of Shimeah David’s brother: and Jonadab was a very subtil man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit ad eum : Quare sic attenuaris macie, fili regis, per singulos dies ? cur non indicas mihi ? Dixitque ei Amnon : Thamar sororem fratris mei Absalom amo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ מַדּ֣וּעַ אַ֠תָּ֠ה כָּ֣כָה דַּ֤ל בֶּן־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ בַּבֹּ֣קֶר בַּבֹּ֔קֶר הֲל֖וֹא תַּגִּ֣יד לִ֑י וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ אַמְנ֔וֹן אֶת־תָּמָ֗ר אֲח֛וֹת אַבְשָׁלֹ֥ם אָחִ֖י אֲנִ֥י אֹהֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to him: Why dost thou grow so lean from day to day, O son of the king? why dost thou not tell me the reason of it? And Ammon said to him: I am in love with Thamar the sister of my brother Absalom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi hänelle: kuninkaan poika, miksis niin laihaksi tulet päivä päivältä? Etkös minulle sitä sano? Niin sanoi Amnon hänelle: minä rakastan Tamaria,veljeni Absalomin sisarta.
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Why art thou, being the king’s son, lean from day to day? wilt thou not tell me? And Amnon said unto him, I love Tamar, my brother Absalom’s sister.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui respondit Jonadab : Cuba super lectum tuum, et languorem simula : cumque venerit pater tuus ut visitet te, dic ei : Veniat, oro, Thamar soror mea, ut det mihi cibum, et faciat pulmentum, ut comedam de manu ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ יְה֣וֹנָדָ֔ב שְׁכַ֥ב עַל־מִשְׁכָּבְךָ֖ וְהִתְחָ֑ל וּבָ֧א אָבִ֣יךָ לִרְאוֹתֶ֗ךָ וְאָמַרְתָּ֣ אֵלָ֡יו תָּ֣בֹא נָא֩ תָמָ֨ר אֲחוֹתִ֜י וְתַבְרֵ֣נִי לֶ֗חֶם וְעָשְׂתָ֤ה לְעֵינַי֙ אֶת־הַבִּרְיָ֔ה לְמַ֙עַן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אֶרְאֶ֔ה וְאָכַלְתִּ֖י מִיָּדָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonadab said to him: Lie down upon thy bed, and feign thyself sick: and when thy father shall come to visit thee, say to him: Let my sister Thamar, I pray thee, come to me, to give me to eat, and to make me a mess, that I may eat it at her hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jonadab sanoi hänelle: laske sinus vuoteeses ja teeskele itses sairaaksi: kuin siis sinun isäs tulee katsomaan sinua, niin sano hänelle: tulkaan sisareni Tamar ruokkimaan minua ja valmistamaan ruokaa minun eteeni, että minä näkisin ja söisin hänen kädestänsä.
verses.king_james: And Jonadab said unto him, Lay thee down on thy bed, and make thyself sick: and when thy father cometh to see thee, say unto him, I pray thee, let my sister Tamar come, and give me meat, and dress the meat in my sight, that I may see it, and eat it at her hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accubuit itaque Amnon, et quasi ægrotare cœpit : cumque venisset rex ad visitandum eum, ait Amnon ad regem : Veniat, obsecro, Thamar soror mea, ut faciat in oculis meis duas sorbitiunculas, et cibum capiam de manu ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֥ב אַמְנ֖וֹן וַיִּתְחָ֑ל וַיָּבֹ֨א הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ לִרְאוֹת֗וֹ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אַמְנ֤וֹן אֶל־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ תָּבוֹא־נָ֞א תָּמָ֣ר אֲחֹתִ֗י וּתְלַבֵּ֤ב לְעֵינַי֙ שְׁתֵּ֣י לְבִב֔וֹת וְאֶבְרֶ֖ה מִיָּדָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Ammon lay down, and made as if he were sick: and when the king came to visit him, Ammon said to the king: I pray thee let my sister Thamar come, and make in my sight two little messes, that I may eat at her hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Amnon pani levätä ja teeskeli itsensä sairaaksi. Kuin kuningas tuli häntä katsomaan, sanoi Amnon kuninkaalle: tulkaan minun sisareni Tamar valmistamaan minun eteeni kaksikin herkkua, että minä söisin hänen kädestänsä.
verses.king_james: So Amnon lay down, and made himself sick: and when the king was come to see him, Amnon said unto the king, I pray thee, let Tamar my sister come, and make me a couple of cakes in my sight, that I may eat at her hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit ergo David ad Thamar domum, dicens : Veni in domum Amnon fratris tui, et fac ei pulmentum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֥ח דָּוִ֛ד אֶל־תָּמָ֖ר הַבַּ֣יְתָה לֵאמֹ֑ר לְכִ֣י נָ֗א בֵּ֚ית אַמְנ֣וֹן אָחִ֔יךְ וַעֲשִׂי־ל֖וֹ הַבִּרְיָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then David sent home to Thamar, saying: Come to the house of thy brother Ammon, and make him a mess.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lähetti David sanan Tamarille huoneesen ja antoi hänelle sanoa: mene veljes Amnonin huoneesen ja valmista hänelle ruokaa.
verses.king_james: Then David sent home to Tamar, saying, Go now to thy brother Amnon’s house, and dress him meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venitque Thamar in domum Amnon fratris sui : ille autem jacebat. Quæ tollens farinam commiscuit, et liquefaciens, in oculis ejus coxit sorbitiunculas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֣לֶךְ תָּמָ֗ר בֵּ֛ית אַמְנ֥וֹן אָחִ֖יהָ וְה֣וּא שֹׁכֵ֑ב וַתִּקַּ֨ח אֶת־הַבָּצֵ֤ק (ותלוש) [וַתָּ֙לׇשׁ֙] וַתְּלַבֵּ֣ב לְעֵינָ֔יו וַתְּבַשֵּׁ֖ל אֶת־הַלְּבִבֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Thamar came to the house of Ammon her brother: but he was laid down: and she took meal and tempered it: and dissolving it in his sight she made little messes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tamar meni veljensä Amnonin huoneesen, ja hän makasi vuoteessa; ja hän otti taikinan, sotkui ja valmisti sen hänen silmäinsä edessä, ja kypsensi ne herkut.
verses.king_james: So Tamar went to her brother Amnon’s house; and he was laid down. And she took flour, and kneaded it, and made cakes in his sight, and did bake the cakes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tollensque quod coxerat, effudit, et posuit coram eo, et noluit comedere : dixitque Amnon : Ejicite universos a me. Cumque ejecissent omnes,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקַּ֤ח אֶת־הַמַּשְׂרֵת֙ וַתִּצֹ֣ק לְפָנָ֔יו וַיְמָאֵ֖ן לֶאֱכ֑וֹל וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אַמְנ֗וֹן הוֹצִ֤יאוּ כׇל־אִישׁ֙ מֵעָלַ֔י וַיֵּצְא֥וּ כׇל־אִ֖ישׁ מֵעָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And taking what she had boiled, she poured it out, and set it before him, but he would not eat: and Ammon said: Put out all persons from me. And when they had put all persons out,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän otti pannun ja kaasi hänen eteensä, vaan ei hän tahtonut syödä. Ja Amnon sanoi: antakaat kaikki mennä ulos minun tyköäni; niin menivät kaikki ulos hänen tyköänsä.
verses.king_james: And she took a pan, and poured them out before him; but he refused to eat. And Amnon said, Have out all men from me. And they went out every man from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit Amnon ad Thamar : Infer cibum in conclave, ut vescar de manu tua. Tulit ergo Thamar sorbitiunculas quas fecerat, et intulit ad Amnon fratrem suum in conclave.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אַמְנ֜וֹן אֶל־תָּמָ֗ר הָבִ֤יאִי הַבִּרְיָה֙ הַחֶ֔דֶר וְאֶבְרֶ֖ה מִיָּדֵ֑ךְ וַתִּקַּ֣ח תָּמָ֗ר אֶת־הַלְּבִבוֹת֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֔תָה וַתָּבֵ֛א לְאַמְנ֥וֹן אָחִ֖יהָ הֶחָֽדְרָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Ammon said to Thamar: Bring the mess into the chamber, that I may eat at thy hand. And Thamar took the little messes which she had made, and brought them in to her brother Ammon in the chamber.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Amnon Tamarille: kanna ruoka kamariin syödäkseni kädestäs. Niin Tamar otti herkun, jonka hän valmistanut oli, ja kantoi kamariin veljensä Amnonin tykö.
verses.king_james: And Amnon said unto Tamar, Bring the meat into the chamber, that I may eat of thine hand. And Tamar took the cakes which she had made, and brought them into the chamber to Amnon her brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque obtulisset ei cibum, apprehendit eam, et ait : Veni, cuba mecum, soror mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּגֵּ֥שׁ אֵלָ֖יו לֶאֱכֹ֑ל וַיַּֽחֲזֶק־בָּהּ֙ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לָ֔הּ בּ֛וֹאִי שִׁכְבִ֥י עִמִּ֖י אֲחוֹתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when she had presented him the meat, he took hold of her, and said: Come lie with me, my sister.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän taritsi hänelle syödä, tarttui hän häneen ja sanoi hänelle: tule minun sisareni ja makaa minun kanssani.
verses.king_james: And when she had brought them unto him to eat, he took hold of her, and said unto her, Come lie with me, my sister.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ respondit ei : Noli frater mi, noli opprimere me : neque enim hoc fas est in Israël : noli facere stultitiam hanc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ אַל־אָחִי֙ אַל־תְּעַנֵּ֔נִי כִּ֛י לֹא־יֵעָשֶׂ֥ה כֵ֖ן בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל אַֽל־תַּעֲשֵׂ֖ה אֶת־הַנְּבָלָ֥ה הַזֹּֽאת׃
verses.douay_rheims: She answered him: Do not so, my brother, do not force me: for no such thing must be done in Israel. Do not thou this folly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi: ei, minun veljeni, älä minua häpäise! sillä ei niin pidä tehtämän Israelissa. älä tee hulluutta.
verses.king_james: And she answered him, Nay, my brother, do not force me; for no such thing ought to be done in Israel: do not thou this folly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego enim ferre non potero opprobrium meum, et tu eris quasi unus de insipientibus in Israël : quin potius loquere ad regem, et non negabit me tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֗י אָ֤נָה אוֹלִיךְ֙ אֶת־חֶרְפָּתִ֔י וְאַתָּ֗ה תִּֽהְיֶ֛ה כְּאַחַ֥ד הַנְּבָלִ֖ים בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְעַתָּה֙ דַּבֶּר־נָ֣א אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ כִּ֛י לֹ֥א יִמְנָעֵ֖נִי מִמֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I shall not be able to bear my shame, and thou shalt be as one of the fools in Israel: but rather speak to the king, and he will not deny me to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuhunka minun pitäis häpiäni sälyttämän? Ja sinä tulisit niinkuin tyhmä Israelissa. Puhu paremmin kuninkaan kanssa, sillä ei hän minua sinulta kiellä.
verses.king_james: And I, whither shall I cause my shame to go? and as for thee, thou shalt be as one of the fools in Israel. Now therefore, I pray thee, speak unto the king; for he will not withhold me from thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noluit autem acquiescere precibus ejus, sed prævalens viribus oppressit eam, et cubavit cum ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֥א אָבָ֖ה לִשְׁמֹ֣עַ בְּקוֹלָ֑הּ וַיֶּחֱזַ֤ק מִמֶּ֙נָּה֙ וַיְעַנֶּ֔הָ וַיִּשְׁכַּ֖ב אֹתָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he would not hearken to her prayers, but being stronger overpowered her and lay with her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta ei hän tahtonut kuulla häntä, vaan voitti hänen, häpäisi ja makasi hänen.
verses.king_james: Howbeit he would not hearken unto her voice: but, being stronger than she, forced her, and lay with her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exosam eam habuit Amnon odio magno nimis : ita ut majus esset odium quo oderat eam, amore quo ante dilexerat. Dixitque ei Amnon : Surge, et vade.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׂנָאֶ֣הָ אַמְנ֗וֹן שִׂנְאָה֙ גְּדוֹלָ֣ה מְאֹ֔ד כִּ֣י גְדוֹלָ֗ה הַשִּׂנְאָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר שְׂנֵאָ֔הּ מֵאַהֲבָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר אֲהֵבָ֑הּ וַיֹּֽאמֶר־לָ֥הּ אַמְנ֖וֹן ק֥וּמִי לֵֽכִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Ammon hated her with an exceeding great hatred: so that the hatred wherewith he hated her was greater than the love with which he had loved her before. And Ammon said to her: Arise, and get thee gone.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte vihasi Amnon häntä sangen suurella vihalla, niin että viha, jolla hän häntä vihasi oli suurempi, kuin rakkaus ennen oli, jolla hän häntä rakasti. Ja Amnon sanoi hänelle: nouse ja mene.
verses.king_james: Then Amnon hated her exceedingly; so that the hatred wherewith he hated her was greater than the love wherewith he had loved her. And Amnon said unto her, Arise, be gone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ respondit ei : Majus est hoc malum quod nunc agis adversum me, quam quod ante fecisti, expellens me. Et noluit audire eam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ אַל־אוֹדֹ֞ת הָרָעָ֤ה הַגְּדוֹלָה֙ הַזֹּ֔את מֵאַחֶ֛רֶת אֲשֶׁר־עָשִׂ֥יתָ עִמִּ֖י לְשַׁלְּחֵ֑נִי וְלֹ֥א אָבָ֖ה לִשְׁמֹ֥עַֽ לָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She answered him: The evil which now thou dost against me, in driving me away, is greater than that which thou didst before. And he would not hearken to her:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi: eikö tämä pahuus ole suurempi kuin se toinen, jonka sinä olet tehnyt minun kanssani, ettäs minun ajat ulos. Mutta ei hän tahtonut kuulla häntä;
verses.king_james: And she said unto him, There is no cause: this evil in sending me away is greater than the other that thou didst unto me. But he would not hearken unto her.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed vocato puero qui ministrabat ei, dixit : Ejice hanc a me foras, et claude ostium post eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֗א אֶֽת־נַעֲרוֹ֙ מְשָׁ֣רְת֔וֹ וַיֹּ֕אמֶר שִׁלְחוּ־נָ֥א אֶת־זֹ֛את מֵעָלַ֖י הַח֑וּצָה וּנְעֹ֥ל הַדֶּ֖לֶת אַחֲרֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But calling the servants that ministered to him, he said: Thrust this woman out from me: and shut the door after her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan kutsui nuorukaisensa, joka häntä palveli ja sanoi: aja tämä ulos minun tyköäni, ja sulje ovi kiinni hänen jälkeensä.
verses.king_james: Then he called his servant that ministered unto him, and said, Put now this woman out from me, and bolt the door after her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ induta erat talari tunica : hujuscemodi enim filiæ regis virgines vestibus utebantur. Ejecit itaque eam minister illius foras : clausitque fores post eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָלֶ֙יהָ֙ כְּתֹ֣נֶת פַּסִּ֔ים כִּי֩ כֵ֨ן תִּלְבַּ֧שְׁןָ בְנוֹת־הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ הַבְּתוּלֹ֖ת מְעִילִ֑ים וַיֹּצֵ֨א אוֹתָ֤הּ מְשָֽׁרְתוֹ֙ הַח֔וּץ וְנָעַ֥ל הַדֶּ֖לֶת אַחֲרֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she was clothed with a long robe: for the king's daughters that were virgins, used such kind of garments. Then his servant thrust her out: and shut the door after her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen yllänsä oli kirjava hame; sillä senkaltaisilla hameilla olivat kuninkaan tyttäret vaatetetut, niinkauvan kuin he olivat neitseet. Ja kuin hänen palveliansa oli hänen ajanut ulos ja sulkenut oven hänen jälkeensä,
verses.king_james: And she had a garment of divers colours upon her: for with such robes were the king’s daughters that were virgins apparelled. Then his servant brought her out, and bolted the door after her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ aspergens cinerem capiti suo, scissa talari tunica, impositisque manibus super caput suum, ibat ingrediens, et clamans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקַּ֨ח תָּמָ֥ר אֵ֙פֶר֙ עַל־רֹאשָׁ֔הּ וּכְתֹ֧נֶת הַפַּסִּ֛ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָלֶ֖יהָ קָרָ֑עָה וַתָּ֤שֶׂם יָדָהּ֙ עַל־רֹאשָׁ֔הּ וַתֵּ֥לֶךְ הָל֖וֹךְ וְזָעָֽקָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she put ashes on her head, and rent her long robe and laid her hands upon her head, and went on crying.
verses.finnish_biblia: Heitti Tamar tuhkaa päänsä päälle, ja repäisi sen kirjavan hameen rikki, joka hänen yllänsä oli, ja pani kätensä päänsä päälle, ja huusi mennessänsä.
verses.king_james: And Tamar put ashes on her head, and rent her garment of divers colours that was on her, and laid her hand on her head, and went on crying.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ei Absalom frater suus : Numquid Amnon frater tuus concubuit tecum ? sed nunc soror, tace : frater tuus est : neque affligas cor tuum pro hac re. Mansit itaque Thamar contabescens in domo Absalom fratris sui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלֶ֜יהָ אַבְשָׁל֣וֹם אָחִ֗יהָ הַאֲמִינ֣וֹן אָחִ֘יךְ֮ הָיָ֣ה עִמָּךְ֒ וְעַתָּ֞ה אֲחוֹתִ֤י הַחֲרִ֙ישִׁי֙ אָחִ֣יךְ ה֔וּא אַל־תָּשִׁ֥יתִי אֶת־לִבֵּ֖ךְ לַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֑ה וַתֵּ֤שֶׁב תָּמָר֙ וְשֹׁ֣מֵמָ֔ה בֵּ֖ית אַבְשָׁל֥וֹם אָחִֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom her brother said to her: Hath thy brother Ammon lain with thee? but now, sister, hold thy peace, he is thy brother: and afflict not thy heart for this thing. So Thamar remained pining away in the house of Absalom her brother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen veljensä Absalom sanoi hänelle: onko sinun veljes Amnon ollut sinun kanssas? Ole ääneti sisareni: se on sinun veljes, älä sitä niin pane sydämees. Niin Tamar jäi yksinäiseksi veljensä Absalomin huoneesen.
verses.king_james: And Absalom her brother said unto her, Hath Amnon thy brother been with thee? but hold now thy peace, my sister: he is thy brother; regard not this thing. So Tamar remained desolate in her brother Absalom’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem audisset rex David verba hæc, contristatus est valde : et noluit contristare spiritum Amnon filii sui, quoniam diligebat eum, quia primogenitus erat ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמֶּ֣לֶךְ דָּוִ֔ד שָׁמַ֕ע אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵ֑לֶּה וַיִּ֥חַר ל֖וֹ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when king David heard of these things he was exceedingly grieved: and he would not afflict the spirit of his son Ammon, for he loved him, because he was his firstborn.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin kuningas David kaikki nämät kuuli vihastui hän sangen suuresti.
verses.king_james: But when king David heard of all these things, he was very wroth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro non est locutus Absalom ad Amnon nec malum nec bonum : oderat enim Absalom Amnon, eo quod violasset Thamar sororem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־דִבֶּ֧ר אַבְשָׁל֛וֹם עִם־אַמְנ֖וֹן לְמֵרָ֣ע וְעַד־ט֑וֹב כִּֽי־שָׂנֵ֤א אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ אֶת־אַמְנ֔וֹן עַל־דְּבַר֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עִנָּ֔ה אֵ֖ת תָּמָ֥ר אֲחֹתֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But Absalom spoke not to Ammon neither good nor evil: for Absalom hated Ammon because he had ravished his sister Thamar.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Absalom ei puhunut Amnonille pahaa eli hyvää; vaan Absalom vihasi Amnonia että hän häpäisi hänen sisarensa Tamarin.
verses.king_james: And Absalom spake unto his brother Amnon neither good nor bad: for Absalom hated Amnon, because he had forced his sister Tamar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem post tempus biennii ut tonderentur oves Absalom in Baalhasor, quæ est juxta Ephraim : et vocavit Absalom omnes filios regis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ לִשְׁנָתַ֣יִם יָמִ֔ים וַיִּהְי֤וּ גֹֽזְזִים֙ לְאַבְשָׁל֔וֹם בְּבַ֥עַל חָצ֖וֹר אֲשֶׁ֣ר עִם־אֶפְרָ֑יִם וַיִּקְרָ֥א אַבְשָׁל֖וֹם לְכׇל־בְּנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass after two years, that the sheep of Absalom were shorn in Baalhasor, which is near Ephraim: and Absalom invited all the king's sons:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kahden vuoden perästä antoi Absalom kerittää lampaitansa BaalHatsarissa, joka on Ephraimin tykönä; ja Absalom kutsui kaikki kuninkaan lapset.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after two full years, that Absalom had sheepshearers in Baal–hazor, which is beside Ephraim: and Absalom invited all the king’s sons.

verses.latin_vulgate: venitque ad regem, et ait ad eum : Ecce tondentur oves servi tui : veniat, oro, rex cum servis suis ad servum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֤א אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וַיֹּ֕אמֶר הִנֵּה־נָ֥א גֹזְזִ֖ים לְעַבְדֶּ֑ךָ יֵלֶךְ־נָ֥א הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וַעֲבָדָ֖יו עִם־עַבְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he came to the king, and said to him: Behold thy servant's sheep are shorn. Let the king, I pray, with his servants come to his servant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom tuli myös kuninkaan tykö ja sanoi: katso, palveliallas ovat nyt lammasten keritsiät: minä rukoilen, että kuningas palvelioinensa tulis palveliansa tykö.
verses.king_james: And Absalom came to the king, and said, Behold now, thy servant hath sheepshearers; let the king, I beseech thee, and his servants go with thy servant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque rex ad Absalom : Noli fili mi, noli rogare ut veniamus omnes et gravemus te. Cum autem cogeret eum, et noluisset ire, benedixit ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ אֶל־אַבְשָׁל֗וֹם אַל־בְּנִי֙ אַל־נָ֤א נֵלֵךְ֙ כֻּלָּ֔נוּ וְלֹ֥א נִכְבַּ֖ד עָלֶ֑יךָ וַיִּפְרׇץ־בּ֛וֹ וְלֹא־אָבָ֥ה לָלֶ֖כֶת וַֽיְבָרְכֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Absalom: Nay, my son, do not ask that we should all come, and be chargeable to thee. And when he pressed him, and he would not go, he blessed him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuningas sanoi Absalomille: ei niin, poikani, älkäämme kaikki menkö, ettemme tekisi sinulle vaivaa. Ja vaikka hän suuresti rukoili häntä, ei hän kuitenkaan tahtonut mennä, vaan siunasi häntä.
verses.king_james: And the king said to Absalom, Nay, my son, let us not all now go, lest we be chargeable unto thee. And he pressed him: howbeit he would not go, but blessed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Absalom : Si non vis venire, veniat, obsecro, nobiscum saltem Amnon frater meus. Dixitque ad eum rex : Non est necesse ut vadat tecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם וָלֹ֕א יֵלֶךְ־נָ֥א אִתָּ֖נוּ אַמְנ֣וֹן אָחִ֑י וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ לָ֥מָּה יֵלֵ֖ךְ עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom said: If thou wilt not come, at least let my brother Ammon, I beseech thee, come with us. And the king said to him: It is not necessary that he should go with thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Absalom sanoi: jollet sinä tule, niin salli veljeni Amnonin käydä meidän kanssamme. Kuningas sanoi hänelle: mitä hän menee sinun kanssas?
verses.king_james: Then said Absalom, If not, I pray thee, let my brother Amnon go with us. And the king said unto him, Why should he go with thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Coëgit itaque Absalom eum, et dimisit cum eo Amnon et universos filios regis. Feceratque Absalom convivium quasi convivium regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְרׇץ־בּ֖וֹ אַבְשָׁל֑וֹם וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח אִתּוֹ֙ אֶת־אַמְנ֔וֹן וְאֵ֖ת כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But Absalom pressed him, so that he let Ammon and all the king's sons go with him. And Absalom made a feast as it were the feast of a king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Absalom vaati häntä, että hän antoi Amnonin ja kaikki kuninkaan lapset mennä heidän kanssansa.
verses.king_james: But Absalom pressed him, that he let Amnon and all the king’s sons go with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præceperat autem Absalom pueris suis, dicens : Observate cum temulentus fuerit Amnon vino, et dixero vobis : Percutite eum, et interficite : nolite timere : ego enim sum qui præcipio vobis : roboramini, et estote viri fortes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְצַו֩ אַבְשָׁל֨וֹם אֶת־נְעָרָ֜יו לֵאמֹ֗ר רְא֣וּ נָ֠א כְּט֨וֹב לֵב־אַמְנ֤וֹן בַּיַּ֙יִן֙ וְאָמַרְתִּ֨י אֲלֵיכֶ֜ם הַכּ֧וּ אֶת־אַמְנ֛וֹן וַהֲמִתֶּ֥ם אֹת֖וֹ אַל־תִּירָ֑אוּ הֲל֗וֹא כִּ֤י אָֽנֹכִי֙ צִוִּ֣יתִי אֶתְכֶ֔ם חִזְק֖וּ וִֽהְי֥וּ לִבְנֵי־חָֽיִל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom had commanded his servants, saying: Take notice when Ammon shall be drunk with wine, and when I shall say to you: Strike him, and kill him, fear not: for it is I that command you: take courage, and be valiant men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom käski palvelioillensa ja sanoi: ottakaat vaari kuin Amnon tulee iloiseksi viinasta ja minä sanon teille: lyökäät Amnon! niin tappakaat häntä, älkäät peljätkö, sillä minä olen sen teille käskenyt, olkaat rohkiat ja miehuulliset siihen.
verses.king_james: Now Absalom had commanded his servants, saying, Mark ye now when Amnon’s heart is merry with wine, and when I say unto you, Smite Amnon; then kill him, fear not: have not I commanded you? be courageous, and be valiant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecerunt ergo pueri Absalom adversum Amnon sicut præceperat eis Absalom. Surgentesque omnes filii regis ascenderunt singuli mulas suas, et fugerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיַּעֲשׂ֞וּ נַעֲרֵ֤י אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ לְאַמְנ֔וֹן כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר צִוָּ֖ה אַבְשָׁל֑וֹם וַיָּקֻ֣מוּ ׀ כׇּל־בְּנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ וַֽיִּרְכְּב֛וּ אִ֥ישׁ עַל־פִּרְדּ֖וֹ וַיָּנֻֽסוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the servants of Absalom did to Ammon as Absalom had commanded them. And all the king's sons arose and got up every man upon his mule, and fled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalomin palveliat tekivät Amnonille niinkuin Absalom käskenyt oli. Niin kaikki kuninkaan lapset nousivat, ja jokainen istui muulinsa päälle ja pakeni.
verses.king_james: And the servants of Absalom did unto Amnon as Absalom had commanded. Then all the king’s sons arose, and every man gat him up upon his mule, and fled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque adhuc pergerent in itinere, fama pervenit ad David, dicens : Percussit Absalom omnes filios regis, et non remansit ex eis saltem unus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ הֵ֣מָּה בַדֶּ֔רֶךְ וְהַשְּׁמֻעָ֣ה בָ֔אָה אֶל־דָּוִ֖ד לֵאמֹ֑ר הִכָּ֤ה אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ אֶת־כׇּל־בְּנֵ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ וְלֹא־נוֹתַ֥ר מֵהֶ֖ם אֶחָֽד׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And while they were yet in the way, a rumour came to David, saying: Absalom hath slain all the king's sons, and there is not one them left.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin he vielä tiellä olivat, tuli sanoma Davidille, sanoen: Absalom on lyönyt kaikki kuninkaan lapset, niin ettei yksikään heistä ole jäänyt.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, while they were in the way, that tidings came to David, saying, Absalom hath slain all the king’s sons, and there is not one of them left.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexit itaque rex, et scidit vestimenta sua, et cecidit super terram : et omnes servi illius qui assistebant ei, sciderunt vestimenta sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֧קׇם הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וַיִּקְרַ֥ע אֶת־בְּגָדָ֖יו וַיִּשְׁכַּ֣ב אָ֑רְצָה וְכׇל־עֲבָדָ֥יו נִצָּבִ֖ים קְרֻעֵ֥י בְגָדִֽים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Then the king rose up, and rent his garments: and fell upon the ground, and all his servants, that stood about him, rent their garments.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin nousi kuningas ja repäisi vaatteensa rikki, ja heitti itsensä maahan; ja kaikki palveliat, jotka seisoivat hänen ympärillänsä, repäisivät vaatteensa rikki.
verses.king_james: Then the king arose, and tare his garments, and lay on the earth; and all his servants stood by with their clothes rent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Jonadab filius Semmaa fratris David, dixit : Ne æstimet dominus meus rex quod omnes pueri filii regis occisi sint : Amnon solus mortuus est, quoniam in ore Absalom erat positus ex die qua oppressit Thamar sororem ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֡עַן יוֹנָדָ֣ב ׀ בֶּן־שִׁמְעָ֨ה אֲחִי־דָוִ֜ד וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אַל־יֹאמַ֤ר אֲדֹנִי֙ אֵ֣ת כׇּל־הַנְּעָרִ֤ים בְּנֵֽי־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ הֵמִ֔יתוּ כִּֽי־אַמְנ֥וֹן לְבַדּ֖וֹ מֵ֑ת כִּֽי־עַל־פִּ֤י אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ הָיְתָ֣ה שׂוּמָ֔ה מִיּוֹם֙ עַנֹּת֔וֹ אֵ֖ת תָּמָ֥ר אֲחֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Jonadab the son of Semmaa David's brother answering, said: Let not my lord the king think that all the king's sons are slain: Ammon only is dead, for he was appointed by the mouth of Absalom from the day that he ravished his sister Thamar.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasi Jonadab Simean Davidin veljen poika ja sanoi: älköön minun Herrani ajatelko, että kaikki nuorukaiset kuninkaan lapsista ovat tapetut, vaan ainoastansa Amnon on kuollut; sillä se on ollut Absalomin mielessä siitä päivästä, kuin hän häpäisi hänen sisarensa Tamarin.
verses.king_james: And Jonadab, the son of Shimeah David’s brother, answered and said, Let not my lord suppose that they have slain all the young men the king’s sons; for Amnon only is dead: for by the appointment of Absalom this hath been determined from the day that he forced his sister Tamar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo ne ponat dominus meus rex super cor suum verbum istud, dicens : Omnes filii regis occisi sunt : quoniam Amnon solus mortuus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֡ה אַל־יָשֵׂם֩ אֲדֹנִ֨י הַמֶּ֤לֶךְ אֶל־לִבּוֹ֙ דָּבָ֣ר לֵאמֹ֔ר כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ מֵ֑תוּ כִּֽי־(אם)־אַמְנ֥וֹן לְבַדּ֖וֹ מֵֽת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore let not my lord the king take this thing into his heart, saying: All the king's sons are slain: for Ammon only is dead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin älköön herrani kuningas panko sydämellensä, ajatellen, että kaikki kuninkaan lapset ovat kuolleet; sillä Amnon on ainoastansa kuollut.
verses.king_james: Now therefore let not my lord the king take the thing to his heart, to think that all the king’s sons are dead: for Amnon only is dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugit autem Absalom. Et elevavit puer speculator oculos suos, et aspexit : et ecce populus multus veniebat per iter devium ex latere montis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּבְרַ֖ח אַבְשָׁל֑וֹם וַיִּשָּׂ֞א הַנַּ֤עַר הַצֹּפֶה֙ אֶת־עֵינָ֔ו וַיַּ֗רְא וְהִנֵּ֨ה עַם־רַ֜ב הֹלְכִ֥ים מִדֶּ֛רֶךְ אַחֲרָ֖יו מִצַּ֥ד הָהָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Absalom fled away: and the young man that kept the watch, lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold there came much people by a by-way on the side of the mountain.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom pakeni; ja palvelia, joka vartiana oli, nosti silmänsä ja näki, ja katso, suuri joukko kansaa tuli tiellä toinen toisensa perästä, vuoren sivulta.
verses.king_james: But Absalom fled. And the young man that kept the watch lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, there came much people by the way of the hill side behind him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Jonadab ad regem : Ecce filii regis adsunt : juxta verbum servi tui, sic factum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יֽוֹנָדָב֙ אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ הִנֵּ֥ה בְנֵי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ בָּ֑אוּ כִּדְבַ֥ר עַבְדְּךָ֖ כֵּ֥ן הָיָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonadab said to the king: Behold the king's sons are come: as thy servant said, so it is.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jonadab sanoi kuninkaalle: katso, kuninkaan lapset tulevat; niinkuin palvelias sanonut on, niin on myös tapahtunut.
verses.king_james: And Jonadab said unto the king, Behold, the king’s sons come: as thy servant said, so it is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque cessasset loqui, apparuerunt et filii regis : et intrantes levaverunt vocem suam, et fleverunt : sed et rex et omnes servi ejus fleverunt ploratu magno nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י ׀ כְּכַלֹּת֣וֹ לְדַבֵּ֗ר וְהִנֵּ֤ה בְנֵֽי־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ בָּ֔אוּ וַיִּשְׂא֥וּ קוֹלָ֖ם וַיִּבְכּ֑וּ וְגַם־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ וְכׇל־עֲבָדָ֔יו בָּכ֕וּ בְּכִ֖י גָּד֥וֹל מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he made an end of speaking, the king's sons also appeared: and coming in they lifted up their voice, and wept: and the king also and all his servants wept very much.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän puhunut oli, katso, kuninkaan lapset tulivat, korottivat äänensä ja itkivät. Kuningas ja kaikki hänen palveliansa itkivät myös suurella itkulla.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as soon as he had made an end of speaking, that, behold, the king’s sons came, and lifted up their voice and wept: and the king also and all his servants wept very sore.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Absalom fugiens abiit ad Tholomai filium Ammiud regem Gessur. Luxit ergo David filium suum cunctis diebus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַבְשָׁל֣וֹם בָּרַ֔ח וַיֵּ֛לֶךְ אֶל־תַּלְמַ֥י בֶּן־[עַמִּיה֖וּד] (עמיחור) מֶ֣לֶךְ גְּשׁ֑וּר וַיִּתְאַבֵּ֥ל עַל־בְּנ֖וֹ כׇּל־הַיָּמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Absalom fled, and went to Tholomai the son of Ammiud the king of Gessur. And David mourned for his son every day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Albsalom pakeni ja meni Talmain Gessurin kuninkaan Ammihudin pojan tykö; mutta hän murehti poikansa tähden ne päivät.
verses.king_james: But Absalom fled, and went to Talmai, the son of Ammihud, king of Geshur. And David mourned for his son every day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Absalom autem cum fugisset, et venisset in Gessur, fuit ibi tribus annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַבְשָׁל֥וֹם בָּרַ֖ח וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ גְּשׁ֑וּר וַֽיְהִי־שָׁ֖ם שָׁלֹ֥שׁ שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom after he was fled, and come into Gessur, was there three years. And king David ceased to pursue after Absalom, because he was comforted concerning the death of Ammon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta sittekuin Absalom oli paennut ja matkustanut Gessuriin oli hän siellä kolme ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: So Absalom fled, and went to Geshur, and was there three years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cessavitque rex David persequi Absalom, eo quod consolatus esset super Amnon interitu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתְּכַל֙ דָּוִ֣ד הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ לָצֵ֖את אֶל־אַבְשָׁל֑וֹם כִּי־נִחַ֥ם עַל־אַמְנ֖וֹן כִּי־מֵֽת׃ {ס}
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sen jälkeen lakkasi kuningas David menemästä Absalomia vastaan; sillä hän oli jo lohdutettu Amnonin jälkeen, joka kuollut oli.
verses.king_james: And the soul of king David longed to go forth unto Absalom: for he was comforted concerning Amnon, seeing he was dead.
